sanskrit: अज्ञश्चाश्रद्दधानश्च संशयात्मा विनश्यति । नायं लोकोऽस्ति न परो न सुखं संशयात्मनः ॥
hindiTranslation: विवेकहीन और श्रद्धारहित संशयात्मा मनुष्यका पतन हो जाता है ।  ऐसे संशयात्मा मनुष्यके लिये न यह लोक है न परलोक है और न सुख ही है ॥
englishTranslation: The ignorant and faithless person who doubts is ruined. For the doubter, there is neither this world, nor the next, nor happiness.
essence: A divided mind loses both peace and direction.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: A person who lacks clear understanding and trust, and keeps doubting, falls apart. Such a divided mind finds no real happiness here or beyond.
simpleInsight: You feel the cost of indecision: a mind that won't commit cannot settle anywhere.
detailedMeaning: Krishna is still answering Arjuna on the battlefield, where hesitation can decide everything. After saying knowledge comes through trust and discipline, he warns that a doubting, faithless mind collapses and cannot find peace in any world.
modernRelevance: You keep reopening the same message before sending it, afraid of being wrong. The delay is not caution anymore — it is the split mind making you smaller.
translationLiteral: अज्ञः (ignorant) / च (and) / अश्रद्दधानः (without trust) / च (and) / संशयात्मा (a doubting inner being) / विनश्यति (is ruined) / न (not) / अयम् (this) / लोकः (world) / अस्ति (exists) / न (not) / परः (other) / न (not) / सुखम् (happiness) / संशयात्मनः (of the doubting inner being)
